question_text: A school uses an electromagnet to separate iron cans from a pile of mixed waste materials. Explain two advantages of using an electromagnet for this task instead of a permanent magnet.
correct_answer: Any two of: (1) The electromagnet can be switched on and off, so cans can be picked up when the magnet is on and released when it is switched off; (2) The strength of the electromagnet can be controlled by changing the current, allowing different weights of cans to be lifted; (3) The electromagnet does not need to be physically removed from the pile like a permanent magnet would.
explanation: Electromagnets are more practical for industrial separation because they can be controlled—turned on to attract iron and off to release it—whereas a permanent magnet would continuously attract iron and be difficult to separate from the material. The ability to control the strength is also useful for handling different loads.
